steps.title: Identify what the blank needs
steps.content: The blank fills the slot before “findings” and must function as a possessive determiner referring to “Mercer and Wright” — a plural noun phrase. The correct word must be a plural possessive, not a contraction.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, keep the right one
steps.content:
• Choice A — They're: Violates possessive vs. contraction — “they’re” is a contraction for “they are,” not a possessive determiner, so it produces a grammatically broken sentence. ✗
  • Choice B — It's: Violates possessive vs. contraction — “it’s” is a contraction for “it is” or “it has,” not a possessive determiner, and also does not agree with the plural antecedent. ✗
  • Choice C — Their: “Their” is the plural possessive determiner, correctly agreeing in number with the plural antecedent “Mercer and Wright.” ✓
  • Choice D — Its: Violates pronoun-antecedent agreement — “its” is a singular possessive determiner and does not agree with the plural “Mercer and Wright.” ✗
C is correct.
